message:
fix: component rendering correctness and performance improvements

BeadsView (beads-view.tsx):
- Parallelize SQLite chunk downloads using Promise.all() instead of
  sequential await-in-loop. All chunks are fetched concurrently, with
  a shared counter tracking completed downloads for the progress
  message ("Downloading Neural Chunks (3/8)..."). This significantly
  reduces total load time on high-latency connections where round-trip
  overhead dominated.

FrankenEye (franken-eye.tsx):
- Gate the random blink animation on isVisibleRef.current so the eye
  only blinks when the component is actually in the viewport. Without
  this check, the setInterval would trigger setState calls even when
  the element was scrolled off-screen, causing unnecessary re-renders.

FrankenGlitch (franken-glitch.tsx):
- Wrap glitchVariants object definition in useMemo(…, [intensity]) so
  the Framer Motion variants object is only recreated when the
  intensity prop changes, not on every render. Previously, a new
  object reference was created each render, which could cause Framer
  Motion to re-evaluate animations unnecessarily.

GlowOrbits (glow-orbits.tsx):
- Replace fixed-range useTransform([0, 1000], [20, -20]) with a
  viewport-relative calculation that normalizes mouse position against
  window.innerWidth/innerHeight. The old mapping assumed a 1000px
  viewport; the new one produces correct parallax at any screen size.
- Add { passive: true } to the mousemove event listener to signal the
  browser that the handler won't call preventDefault(), enabling
  compositor-thread scrolling optimizations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
